course.title: Modern Docker 29: Containers, Compose, BuildKit, and Secure Delivery
course.description: Developers and operators who can use Linux and need to build, test, secure, debug, publish, and defend production-shaped container applications. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate docker engine 29.6.1 architecture and lifecycle, oci image/runtime/distribution boundaries, images, containers, registries, storage, networking, security, diagnostics, resource constraints, and daemon operations through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate dockerfile 1.25, buildkit 0.31.1, buildx 0.35.0, build contexts, multi-stage and multi-platform builds, cache, secrets, bake, attestations, reproducibility, policies, and release evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate compose specification with compose 5.3.1 services, dependencies, health, networks, volumes, configs, secrets, profiles, watch, overrides, tests, faults, and production-boundary decisions through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.
misconceptions: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish a container, image, process, namespace, cgroup, virtual machine, and host kernel without overstating isolation.
misconceptions: Every container includes and boots a private guest kernel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish a container, image, process, namespace, cgroup, virtual machine, and host kernel without overstating isolation.

competencies.statement: Separate OCI image, runtime, and distribution contracts from Docker-specific implementation and product behavior.
misconceptions: OCI defines every Docker CLI, Compose, networking, and build feature.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate OCI image, runtime, and distribution contracts from Docker-specific implementation and product behavior.

competencies.statement: Compare Docker Engine and Docker Desktop host, VM, filesystem, networking, licensing, and Linux-versus-Windows container boundaries.
misconceptions: Docker Desktop and native Linux Engine have identical host and filesystem behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare Docker Engine and Docker Desktop host, VM, filesystem, networking, licensing, and Linux-versus-Windows container boundaries.

competencies.statement: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.
misconceptions: A docker --version string proves the client is connected to the intended compatible daemon.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.

competencies.statement: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
misconceptions: docker run restarts the same stopped container whenever it is repeated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.

competencies.statement: Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
misconceptions: Every docker run argument replaces the image entrypoint and command in the same way.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.

competencies.statement: Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
misconceptions: Interactive and TTY flags are required for every process that reads standard input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.

competencies.statement: Use ps, inspect, filters, formats, diff, top, port, and wait to query exact container identity and state before mutation.
misconceptions: Human-readable docker ps output is a stable machine interface for scripts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ps, inspect, filters, formats, diff, top, port, and wait to query exact container identity and state before mutation.

competencies.statement: Use labels, explicit identifiers, dry evidence, and dependency checks to remove only intended containers and resources.
misconceptions: docker system prune is the safest first response to any disk or naming problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use labels, explicit identifiers, dry evidence, and dependency checks to remove only intended containers and resources.

competencies.statement: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
misconceptions: Every Dockerfile instruction produces one filesystem layer containing the whole image.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.

competencies.statement: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
misconceptions: A tag is an immutable content checksum suitable for permanent deployment identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.

competencies.statement: Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
misconceptions: Pulling one tag always downloads one architecture-specific image and every layer again.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.

competencies.statement: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.
misconceptions: docker history reveals every original build command and secret with complete provenance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect image metadata, history, labels, users, entrypoint, command, exposed ports, healthcheck, platform, and size without confusing declared intent with runtime truth.

competencies.statement: Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores.
misconceptions: Removing a local image tag deletes the corresponding remote registry artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan tag, digest, push, retention, deletion, garbage-collection, and rollback evidence across local and remote stores.

competencies.statement: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
misconceptions: The syntax directive is a comment with no effect on available Dockerfile features.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

competencies.statement: Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
misconceptions: .gitignore automatically controls every Docker build context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.

competencies.statement: Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
misconceptions: ADD and COPY are interchangeable and may read any host path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.

competencies.statement: Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.
misconceptions: Shell-form and exec-form commands have identical parsing, environment expansion, and PID behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.

competencies.statement: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.
misconceptions: Multiple CMD instructions combine into a sequence of default commands.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

competencies.statement: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
misconceptions: BuildKit executes every Dockerfile line sequentially and always emits a local image.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.

competencies.statement: Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.
misconceptions: A cache hit proves remote dependencies and package indexes are current.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.

competencies.statement: Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
misconceptions: Multi-stage builds automatically make every stage smaller and secure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use bind, cache, tmpfs, secret, and SSH RUN mounts with scoped lifetime, ownership, and cache behavior.
misconceptions: Build arguments and ENV are safe ways to pass credentials because later RUN steps can unset them.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bind, cache, tmpfs, secret, and SSH RUN mounts with scoped lifetime, ownership, and cache behavior.

competencies.statement: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.
misconceptions: A remote cache makes builds bit-for-bit reproducible and safe to trust without source verification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.

competencies.statement: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
misconceptions: Docker always forwards every signal through any shell wrapper and reaps every child automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.

competencies.statement: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
misconceptions: A successful docker stop proves the application completed all in-flight work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.

competencies.statement: Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.
misconceptions: An unhealthy container is automatically restarted by Docker in every configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.

competencies.statement: Choose no, on-failure, always, or unless-stopped behavior while accounting for manual stops, daemon restarts, and dependency recovery.
misconceptions: A restart policy preserves ordering and makes every application failure safe to retry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose no, on-failure, always, or unless-stopped behavior while accounting for manual stops, daemon restarts, and dependency recovery.

competencies.statement: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.
misconceptions: Containers receive safe CPU and memory limits by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.

competencies.statement: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.
misconceptions: The container writable layer is durable application storage that follows a replacement container.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.

competencies.statement: Create, inspect, mount, share, label, back up, restore, migrate, and remove named and anonymous volumes with explicit ownership.
misconceptions: Removing a container always removes every named and anonymous volume it used.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, inspect, mount, share, label, back up, restore, migrate, and remove named and anonymous volumes with explicit ownership.

competencies.statement: Use bind mounts with deliberate host path, existence, read-only mode, propagation, SELinux, UID/GID, and portability boundaries.
misconceptions: A bind mount is isolated from the host and cannot overwrite host data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bind mounts with deliberate host path, existence, read-only mode, propagation, SELinux, UID/GID, and portability boundaries.

competencies.statement: Choose tmpfs for bounded ephemeral or sensitive data while accounting for memory, swap, permissions, size, and restart loss.
misconceptions: tmpfs guarantees secrets can never reach disk under any host configuration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose tmpfs for bounded ephemeral or sensitive data while accounting for memory, swap, permissions, size, and restart loss.

competencies.statement: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.
misconceptions: A successful archive command proves the application can restore consistent data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.

competencies.statement: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
misconceptions: A Docker bridge is the same object and trust boundary as a physical Ethernet switch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.

competencies.statement: Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
misconceptions: localhost inside one container reaches sibling containers and the host automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.

competencies.statement: Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
misconceptions: EXPOSE publishes a port to the host and internet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.

competencies.statement: Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.
misconceptions: Multiple Compose networks automatically enforce application-layer authorization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.

competencies.statement: Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence.
misconceptions: One successful ping proves DNS, TCP, TLS, application readiness, and published-port policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.
misconceptions: Compose file version 3 is newer and more capable than the merged Compose Specification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.

competencies.statement: Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.
misconceptions: Environment variables, env_file values, and Compose interpolation use one precedence rule.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.

competencies.statement: Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.
misconceptions: Specifying both build and image always causes Compose to build and ignore registry state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.

competencies.statement: Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness.
misconceptions: Short depends_on waits until every dependency is ready to serve requests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness.

competencies.statement: Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.
misconceptions: docker compose down removes every named volume and built image by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.

competencies.statement: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.
misconceptions: Multiple Compose files concatenate every field and resolve every path relative to its own file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.

competencies.statement: Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
misconceptions: A profiled service is never started when explicitly targeted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.

competencies.statement: Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.
misconceptions: Compose Watch replaces application-level hot reload and always works through bind mounts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.

competencies.statement: Choose run, exec, up, wait, exit-code-from, abort-on-exit, attach, and logs for repeatable test and migration jobs.
misconceptions: docker compose run and exec create and target the same process and container state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose run, exec, up, wait, exit-code-from, abort-on-exit, attach, and logs for repeatable test and migration jobs.

competencies.statement: Use config, profiles, images, models, variables, consistency checks, dry-run, and changed-file review before mutating a stack.
misconceptions: YAML parsing success proves a Compose application is valid, portable, and safe to apply.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use config, profiles, images, models, variables, consistency checks, dry-run, and changed-file review before mutating a stack.

competencies.statement: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.
misconceptions: Any process inside a container is safe to run because namespaces prevent host impact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.

competencies.statement: Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.
misconceptions: USER app guarantees the process is unprivileged on every host and mounted path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.

competencies.statement: Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
misconceptions: --privileged is a convenient compatibility switch with only container-local effects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.

competencies.statement: Retain or tighten seccomp, AppArmor or SELinux, no-new-privileges, read-only roots, masked paths, and writable tmpfs exceptions.
misconceptions: Running as non-root makes seccomp, mandatory access control, and capability policy unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain or tighten seccomp, AppArmor or SELinux, no-new-privileges, read-only roots, masked paths, and writable tmpfs exceptions.

competencies.statement: Compare rootless mode, userns remap, rootful daemon groups, contexts, remote TLS, and socket proxies and document remaining authority.
misconceptions: Membership in the docker group grants ordinary non-administrative access only.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare rootless mode, userns remap, rootful daemon groups, contexts, remote TLS, and socket proxies and document remaining authority.

competencies.statement: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
misconceptions: ARG and ENV are equivalent inputs that disappear when the build completes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.

competencies.statement: Resolve CLI, Compose, env_file, image, shell, quoting, unset, empty, required, default, and literal-dollar environment behavior.
misconceptions: The .env file is automatically injected into every container as runtime environment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve CLI, Compose, env_file, image, shell, quoting, unset, empty, required, default, and literal-dollar environment behavior.

competencies.statement: Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.
misconceptions: Compose secrets are encrypted at rest and dynamically rotated in every implementation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.

competencies.statement: Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure.
misconceptions: Deleting a secret in a later image layer removes it from earlier layer content.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure.

competencies.statement: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.
misconceptions: docker login credentials are always stored encrypted and cannot leak through process or CI output.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.

competencies.statement: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
misconceptions: docker logs always reads every application log regardless of the configured logging driver.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.

competencies.statement: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
misconceptions: The latest container log line is sufficient to identify every Docker lifecycle cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.

competencies.statement: Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
misconceptions: A low docker stats percentage proves the host and application have no resource bottleneck.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose inspect-first evidence, exec, cp, export, temporary toolboxes, debug images, namespace entry, and reproduction without mutating the production image blindly.
misconceptions: Installing tools into a live container is the safest durable way to debug and repair it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose inspect-first evidence, exec, cp, export, temporary toolboxes, debug images, namespace entry, and reproduction without mutating the production image blindly.

competencies.statement: Isolate client, daemon, build, image, process, storage, network, health, application, and host causes with competing hypotheses and a recovery record.
misconceptions: Recreating the container first is efficient because it preserves all causal evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Isolate client, daemon, build, image, process, storage, network, health, application, and host causes with competing hypotheses and a recovery record.

competencies.statement: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
misconceptions: A successful docker build proves Dockerfile quality and production behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.

competencies.statement: Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
misconceptions: docker compose config validates that every referenced image can run and every healthcheck will pass.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.

competencies.statement: Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
misconceptions: Application unit tests make image contract tests redundant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.

competencies.statement: Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.
misconceptions: Starting all services once is adequate multi-container integration evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject slow start, crash, dependency refusal, DNS failure, read-only storage, full disk, termination, and resource limits into an isolated Compose project.

competencies.statement: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.
misconceptions: A local cached success proves another host can pull and reproduce the release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.

competencies.statement: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.
misconceptions: The docker images SIZE column is the exact additional disk and network cost for every host.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.

competencies.statement: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
misconceptions: Reordering Dockerfile lines for more cache hits cannot alter correctness or trust.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.

competencies.statement: Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.
misconceptions: The smallest byte count is always the most secure and operable production image.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.

competencies.statement: Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load.
misconceptions: Container overhead is always negligible and needs no measurement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load.

competencies.statement: Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.
misconceptions: Pinning the base image digest alone guarantees a reproducible image digest.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.

competencies.statement: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
misconceptions: Every Buildx builder shares the daemon image store, cache, platforms, and outputs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.

competencies.statement: Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
misconceptions: A multi-platform tag points directly to one universal root filesystem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.

competencies.statement: Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
misconceptions: A QEMU build result proves native runtime performance and compatibility.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.

competencies.statement: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.
misconceptions: Predefined platform arguments are automatically inherited inside every stage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use BUILDPLATFORM, TARGETPLATFORM, TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, and stage pinning without accidentally executing target binaries on the builder.

competencies.statement: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.
misconceptions: docker buildx bake always builds one target and loads it into the local image store.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define inherited Bake targets, variables, matrices, groups, contexts, cache, attestations, outputs, and policy while preventing accidental pushes.

competencies.statement: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.
misconceptions: A successful push proves every expected platform and attestation is present and trusted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.

competencies.statement: Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
misconceptions: An SBOM proves every component is safe, licensed, used, and free of vulnerabilities.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.

competencies.statement: Produce minimal or maximal SLSA provenance, locate materials and parameters, protect secret values, and verify the statement against source and builder policy.
misconceptions: Provenance automatically proves source review, reproducibility, and builder trust.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce minimal or maximal SLSA provenance, locate materials and parameters, protect secret values, and verify the statement against source and builder policy.

competencies.statement: Evaluate package and base vulnerabilities, exploitability evidence, VEX, exceptions, remediation, rescans, approved inputs, and non-root policy.
misconceptions: Every high-severity CVE makes every image equally exploitable and requires the same response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate package and base vulnerabilities, exploitability evidence, VEX, exceptions, remediation, rescans, approved inputs, and non-root policy.

competencies.statement: Gate tests, builds, policies, multi-platform manifests, signatures, attestations, promotion, deployment digest, rollback, and post-release verification in CI.
misconceptions: Retagging latest is sufficient promotion and rollback evidence for a production release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate tests, builds, policies, multi-platform manifests, signatures, attestations, promotion, deployment digest, rollback, and post-release verification in CI.

competencies.statement: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
misconceptions: Editing daemon.json changes every running container immediately and safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.

competencies.statement: Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
misconceptions: The newest Docker CLI guarantees compatibility with every older daemon and Compose model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.

competencies.statement: Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
misconceptions: Unused according to a prune command means unneeded by every application and recovery plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.

competencies.statement: State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform.
misconceptions: A production Compose file automatically provides the same scheduling and availability model as Kubernetes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform.

competencies.statement: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.
misconceptions: Passing isolated technical checks removes the need for an end-to-end production defense.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.

credential.title: Modern Docker 29: Containers, Compose, BuildKit, and Secure Delivery Certificate

modules.title: Docker Architecture, OCI Boundaries, Isolation, and Environment Evidence
modules.description: A civic data team must decide whether containers solve its reproducibility problem without claiming virtual-machine isolation or ignoring daemon authority. Learners cumulatively produce container architecture and environment evidence map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.
Distinguish a container, image, process, namespace, cgroup, virtual machine, and host kernel without overstating isolation.
Separate OCI image, runtime, and distribution contracts from Docker-specific implementation and product behavior.

modules.title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup
modules.description: A support utility must run repeatedly with explicit inputs, observable exit behavior, bounded names, and cleanup that never deletes unrelated state. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic container lifecycle runbook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.

modules.title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity
modules.description: A release team must prove exactly which immutable multi-layer artifact ran even after a mutable tag moves in the registry. Learners cumulatively produce image identity and layer provenance ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.

modules.title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts
modules.description: A small accessible web application needs a readable Dockerfile whose build inputs, defaults, process model, and runtime files are explicit. Learners cumulatively produce reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.

modules.title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries
modules.description: A CI build is slow, leaks credentials through configuration, and ships compilers that production never needs. Learners cumulatively produce cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain frontend-to-LLB conversion, content-addressed operations, parallel stages, skipped work, builder drivers, and output exporters.
Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.
Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.

modules.title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints
modules.description: A queue worker ignores termination, accumulates zombies, reports healthy while stalled, and can exhaust host memory. Learners cumulatively produce bounded process lifecycle and health policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Create bounded healthchecks with start period, interval, timeout, retries, useful output, and a scope distinct from restart or dependency readiness.

modules.title: Writable Layers, Volumes, Bind Mounts, tmpfs, Ownership, and Recovery
modules.description: A stateful service loses data after replacement, while a developer bind mount hides packaged files and writes host files as root. Learners cumulatively produce storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.
Create, inspect, mount, share, label, back up, restore, migrate, and remove named and anonymous volumes with explicit ownership.
Use bind mounts with deliberate host path, existence, read-only mode, propagation, SELinux, UID/GID, and portability boundaries.

modules.title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy
modules.description: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Learners cumulatively produce least-exposure container network topology through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace container interfaces, network namespaces, veth pairs, bridge networks, routes, NAT, firewall rules, and platform boundaries.
Use user-defined networks, embedded DNS, service names, aliases, attach/detach behavior, and IP churn without hard-coded addresses.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.

modules.title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle
modules.description: A web, worker, and database stack needs one portable declared model with predictable naming, dependencies, health gates, and teardown. Learners cumulatively produce validated multi-service Compose application model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain the current Compose Specification and service, network, volume, config, secret, project, and optional implementation boundaries.
Resolve project name, working directory, files, includes, merges, interpolation, environment sources, and canonical config output.
Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.

modules.title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows
modules.description: Developers need fast source sync and optional debugging while CI needs isolated dependencies, deterministic tests, and no production-only side effects. Learners cumulatively produce development and test Compose workflow matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.
Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.

modules.title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Daemon Authority
modules.description: A third-party document converter handles hostile files but requests root, privileged mode, every device, and the Docker socket. Learners cumulatively produce least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.
Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.
Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.

modules.title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data
modules.description: A service must receive database credentials and rotation-safe settings without baking them into images, logs, history, or source control. Learners cumulatively produce configuration and secret-flow contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
Resolve CLI, Compose, env_file, image, shell, quoting, unset, empty, required, default, and literal-dollar environment behavior.
Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.

modules.title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence
modules.description: A service restarts intermittently with no useful application error while disk, memory, network, and health evidence disagree. Learners cumulatively produce layered Docker incident evidence packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.

modules.title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates
modules.description: A release builds successfully but fails at runtime on a clean host, ships a root default, and loses data when its dependency stalls. Learners cumulatively produce container release test and fault matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.

modules.title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility
modules.description: Build time and image size doubled after dependency changes, while production startup and memory regress despite a warm local cache. Learners cumulatively produce measured build and runtime optimization experiment through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.
Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.

modules.title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs
modules.description: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Learners cumulatively produce multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.

modules.title: Registry Publication, SBOM, Provenance, Vulnerabilities, Policies, and CI Release
modules.description: A regulated service needs evidence for what an image contains, how it was built, which base it used, who approved it, and how rollback selects immutable content. Learners cumulatively produce signed and policy-gated image release packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.
Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
Produce minimal or maximal SLSA provenance, locate materials and parameters, protect secret values, and verify the statement against source and builder policy.

modules.title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone Defense
modules.description: An operations board must approve a production container system through daemon upgrade, disk pressure, backup recovery, credential rotation, and migration pressure. Learners cumulatively produce production Docker operations and release defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Plan Engine, API, containerd, runtime, BuildKit, Buildx, Compose, plugin, storage, and deprecated-feature compatibility across staged upgrade and rollback.
Attribute disk use to images, build cache, writable layers, logs, volumes, and snapshots; preserve owners and backups before scoped cleanup.
